plan_summary.primary_goal: Secure ICANN delegation of the .mars gTLD within 18 months under the 'Builder' strategy, with internal HSM-based DNSSEC control and a viable Year 1 financial base before the 50% revenue pledge re-activates.
plan_summary.modelling_frame: Tests Year 1 financial viability of the registry: whether the upfront budget envelope covers HSM CapEx, base OpEx, and the contingency draw from regulatory/political and string-contention shocks, and whether projected Year 1 revenue covers base overhead before the suspended 50% pledge resumes.

key_values.label: Initial budget envelope
key_values.comment: Plan-committed minimum capital allocation; upper bound is $100M+. Lower bound is the validated viability floor.
key_values.source_text: initial budget envelope between USD 25M and USD 100M or more

key_values.label: Internal HSM CapEx (mandatory reallocation)
key_values.comment: Mandatory $5M-$10M CapEx reallocated from contingency for internal HSM procurement, reversing external DNSSEC delegation.
key_values.source_text: mandatory $5M-$10M CapEx immediately reallocated for internal HSM procurement

key_values.label: Dedicated contingency fund
key_values.comment: Ring-fenced $10M-$20M buffer for legal defense, lobbying, and string-contention auctions; absorbs regulatory and political shocks.

key_values.label: Net revenue pledge to research fund
key_values.comment: 50% of net registry revenue committed to environmental/science fund; currently suspended until $25M+ overhead is covered.
key_values.source_text: net revenue commitment of 50% to scientific research fund

key_values.label: Regulatory objection cost shock
key_values.comment: Stress-test magnitude $5M-$10M extra cost on regulatory objection; drives draw on contingency reserve.
key_values.source_text: Defense budget impact of USD 5M-10M extra cost upon regulatory objection

key_values.label: String contention / compliance cost overrun
key_values.comment: Financial risk overrun of $10M-$20M from string contention or trademark issues; primary contingency draw.
key_values.source_text: Financial risk overrun from string contention or compliance costs is modeled at $10M-$20M

key_values.label: Political backlash extra legal cost
key_values.comment: Stress-test $2M-$4M extra legal cost from political backlash over private planetary namespace operation.
key_values.source_text: Political backlash over private operation causes 3-6 month delays plus $2M-$4M extra legal costs

key_values.label: ICANN review duration
key_values.comment: 18-month time-bound completion target (start to 2027-Nov-02 delegation); sets burn-rate denominator.
key_values.source_text: Within 18 months of project start (Target Completion: 2027-November-02)

derived_questions.question: Is the implied Year 1 registration volume to cover base overhead plausible for a brand-new planetary-namespace gTLD?
derived_questions.why_it_matters: Expert criticism flagged the 50% pledge as unsupported by any volume-price-OpEx model; pledge resumes only after this gate clears.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Average .mars registration price (Year 1)
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Required to compute Year 1 revenue and break-even registration volume against base OpEx plus HSM CapEx.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Anchor on historical new-gTLD wholesale prices ($10-$200/domain) and premium-niche TLDs; bracket widely given novelty.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Year 1 .mars registration volume
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Drives total Year 1 revenue and tests the unverified break-even claim in Expert Criticism Issue 2.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Anchor on first-year volumes of novelty/niche new gTLDs (typically 1k-50k domains in Year 1) with wide spread.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Annual base OpEx ceiling
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Required to compute net revenue and determine whether base overhead is covered before the 50% pledge resumes.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Distill assumption: $5M-$10M OpEx mentioned in Issue 2; bracket against 5-FTE staffing plus Tier 1 cloud DNS infrastructure.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Probability of string contention or formal objection
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Determines whether the $10M-$20M overrun is drawn against contingency; gates contingency_after_shocks calculation.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Anchor on ICANN historical contention rate for novel single-word TLDs (~30-50%); .mars uniqueness elevates likelihood.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Year 1 registry revenue
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Establishes top-line revenue magnitude required for every break-even and pledge-survival test.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Year 1 break-even registration volume
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Expert Criticism Issue 2 demands this; tests whether the implied volume to cover base OpEx is realistic before the 50% pledge resumes.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Year 1 revenue surplus after OpEx and 50% pledge
recommended_first_calculations.formula_hint: y1_revenue_surplus_usd = y1_revenue_usd - annual_opex_usd - revenue_pledge_fraction * max(0, y1_revenue_usd - annual_opex_usd)
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Gate: positive surplus after OpEx AND the 50% pledge means the plan covers base overhead even with the pledge active; negative means the pledge must stay suspended or the operating base drains.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Contingency reserve after expected shocks
recommended_first_calculations.formula_hint: contingency_after_shocks_surplus_usd = contingency_reserve_usd - string_contention_overrun_usd * string_contention_probability - regulatory_shock_cost_usd - political_backlash_cost_usd
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Tests whether the ring-fenced $10M-$20M contingency absorbs the named shocks; positive = the contingency line item survives.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Capital runway surplus over 18-month review
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Tests whether the $25M floor budget covers HSM CapEx plus 18 months of OpEx plus the contingency line item; positive = the floor is viable; negative = the plan requires the upper budget range to survive.

unmodelled_gates.label: ICANN delegation approval
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Without ICANN's Board approving the .mars delegation, no operational scenario matters; this is the binding regulatory authorization the model treats as a fixed yes/no input rather than a sampled probability.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: The registry never operates; all budgeted CapEx and OpEx is sunk cost.

unmodelled_gates.label: Major space agency public endorsement
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: The 'Builder' strategy hinges on securing observational support / non-objection from at least two major space agencies before ICANN review; failure unwinds the political legitimacy claim.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: ICANN review momentum stalls; political backlash risk (Risk 5) realizes at the high end.

unmodelled_gates.label: Sovereign / international body acceptance of private planetary namespace
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Premortem and Expert Criticism repeatedly warn that ITU/UN COPUOS or government objection to private operation of a planetary namespace is an existential gate the financial model cannot test.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Delegation rejected or reversed; the entire 7-year governance pathway collapses.

unmodelled_gates.label: Internal HSM FIPS 140-2 Level 3 certification
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Data Collection Item 3 mandates this certification by Q4 2026 to invalidate the externally-delegated DNSSEC posture; failure forfeits cryptographic sovereignty per Expert Criticism.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Plan reverts to outsourced DNSSEC, re-introducing the existential security risk experts flagged.
